demographics: I am 22 years old. I am female. I am White. I have never been married. I completed 4 years of college (bachelor's degree). My family income is $8,000 to $9,999. I am an independent, close to Democrat. I am liberal. I never attend religious services.

events.event_text: Last year, when I was 21, I participated in a local Black Lives Matter protest. It was my first time attending a demonstration, and I felt connected to a community fighting for justice. We marched peacefully, holding signs and chanting for equality.

When I was 17, I was the only white student to join my Black friend, redundant mediated in class. I experienced firsthand the racial divide and prejudice in our school, but our friendship grew stronger as we stood up for justice together.

Last year, at 21, I adopted a black rescue dog named Equality. He's brought me closer to my community, especially the POC families with dogs at the park who welcomed me with open arms. I've experienced firsthand the power of pets to bridge divides.

When I was 19, I joined my college's chapter of the local food cooperative. I've always cared about sustainability and social justice, and this was a chance to get involved in my community. We grew, harvested, and sold local produce, and I made new friends who shared my values. It was comforting to find a like-minded community on campus.

At 18, I was forwarded an email containing a racist joke from someone in my college club. I was the only Person of Color in the club, and I felt singled out and hurt. I decided to confront the person, sparking a club-wide conversation about anti-racism.

At 17, I decided to volunteer at a local community center to complete my high school service hours. I ended up meeting a bunch of incredible people from different backgrounds, and we formed a tight-knit support group. We've been through a lot together since then, including helping each other navigate personal and family crises.

compressed_summaries.summary: This person, at 17 and 21, actively engaged in racial justice, first by joining a Black friend in a school protest and later by participating in a Black Lives Matter march. At 21, they adopted a black rescue dog, fostering connections with POC families in their community. Additionally, at 19, they joined a college food cooperative, aligning with their values of sustainability and social justice.

This person, at 21, adopted a black rescue dog named Equality, fostering connections with POC families in their community, and experienced firsthand the unifying power of pets. At 19, they joined a college food cooperative, driven by sustainability and social justice values, and found a supportive like-minded community. At 18, they confronted racism within their college club, sparking a club-wide conversation about anti-racism.

persona_card: "Spiritually independent, this 22-year-old White female, a college graduate, is deeply committed to racial justice and social equality, yet maintains a moderate level of moral traditionalism. She advocates for robust economic redistribution and a strong government role, while holding structuralist views on racial inequality. Despite her liberal leanings, she expresses low trust in institutions, and harbors open, welcoming feelings towards social outgroups."
